# DocPlaybook

DocPlaybook is an AI-powered, Git-first CLI for translating Markdown documentation, learning reusable translation memory from human edits, and linting translated docs against that memory.

Primary documentation pages:

- /guide/introduction - product overview, supported integrations, and typical workflow
- /guide/quick-start - install DocPlaybook, initialize config, choose provider, apply framework conventions, and learn the main commands
- /guide/workflow - scripts, hooks, and CI workflow patterns
- /guide/config - workspace config and model settings
- /guide/advanced - Git-first translation and learning behavior
- /guide/agents - agent responsibilities and maintained files
- /guide/memory - global playbook and language memory structure
- /guide/ci - recommended CI usage for shared provider/model setup and centralized cost

Important product notes:

- DocPlaybook is LLM-based and users can choose their own provider.
- Teams often lock provider and model in config for consistent translation quality.
- Translation uses Git-based source diffs, so only the target positions that need regeneration are replaced.
- DocPlaybook maintains a global .docplaybook/playbook.md plus per-language .docplaybook/memories/<target>.md files.
